title: Inconsistent boolean handling in _int_or_none implementations
description:
File: python/src/lichen/client/ip_coap.py line 267-270 vs lci.py lines 761-767

Two different implementations of _int_or_none exist with inconsistent boolean handling:

ip_coap.py (does NOT filter booleans):
```python
def _int_or_none(value: Any) -> int | None:
    if value is None:
        return None
    return int(value)  # True becomes 1, False becomes 0
```

lci.py (filters booleans):
```python
def _int_or_none(value: Any) -> int | None:
    if isinstance(value, bool) or value is None:
        return None  # Booleans return None
    try:
        return int(value)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return None
```

Impact: In Python, bool is a subclass of int. If a CoAP response contains a boolean where an integer is expected:
- ip_coap.py will convert True to 1
- lci.py will return None

This inconsistency could cause subtle bugs if the same data flows through both code paths.

Fix: Standardize on the lci.py behavior (filter booleans) since semantic True/False values shouldn't silently become 1/0.
